interpretation: Initial client UDP tuple before migration.
Server detected the second client path.
Client probes the new path with PATH_CHALLENGE.
Server receives the client's PATH_CHALLENGE on the new path.
Server sends PATH_RESPONSE and challenges the reverse path.
Client receives PATH_RESPONSE and server PATH_CHALLENGE.
Client answers the server's reverse path validation challenge.
Server receives PATH_RESPONSE for the reverse validation challenge.
Client log marks the new path as validated.
Server log marks the new path as validated.
Server marks the connection migrated to the new path.
HTTP/3 request is processed after the migration event.
Final client stats show the old path inactive and the new path active.
Final server stats show the migrated path active.
